title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to double the dollar limitation for the energy efficient home improvement credit with respect to heat pumps, heat pump water heaters, biomass stoves, and boilers.
plain_summary: This bill changes the tax credit that people can get for buying certain energy efficient home equipment. Right now, people can get a tax credit of up to two thousand dollars when they buy things like heat pumps, heat pump water heaters, or biomass stoves and boilers for their homes. The bill would double that limit to four thousand dollars for these specific items. This means people could save more money on their taxes when they buy this energy efficient equipment. The new higher limit would start for taxes filed for the year 2025 and after.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Increased Limitation for Heat Pump and Heat Pump Water Heaters; Biomass Stoves and Boilers
sections.text: This section changes the tax code to increase the dollar limit for certain home energy equipment. The current limit of two thousand dollars will be raised to four thousand dollars. This higher limit applies to tax credits for heat pumps, heat pump water heaters, biomass stoves, and boilers that help make homes more energy efficient. The new four thousand dollar limit will start for tax years that begin after December thirty first two thousand twenty four.
